metadata.title: Reference Class Forecasting: Replacing Hope with Base Rates
metadata.note: CLI video. Reference class forecasting to debias M&A synergy estimates.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.
M&A synergy estimates run 20 to 40 percent above realizations. Reference class forecasting replaces the inside view with base rates from comparable deals.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- Inside view: optimism bias. Reference class: historical base rates. The gap is where value goes.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to apply reference class forecasting to three synergy categories using historical realization rates.

props.command: claude "Apply reference class forecasting: cost synergies=$80M (planning fallacy: realize 85%), revenue synergies=$120M (overconfidence: realize 45%), integration cost=$40M (underestimation: actual 130%). Compute adjusted max deal price vs. $1.1B."

beats.narration_text: The script applies each bias correction: planning fallacy on costs, overconfidence on revenue, and underestimation on integration expenses.

props.code:
standalone=850
bias={"cost":{"inside":80,"rate":0.85},
      "revenue":{"inside":120,"rate":0.45},
      "integ":{"inside":40,"rate":1.30}}
for k,v in bias.items():
    adj=v["inside"]*v["rate"]
    print(f"{k}: inside={v['inside']} "
          f"adj={adj:.0f}")
adj_max=(standalone
    +bias["cost"]["inside"]*0.85
    +bias["revenue"]["inside"]*0.45
    -bias["integ"]["inside"]*1.30)
print(f"Adj max: ${adj_max:.0f}M")
if 1100>adj_max:
    print("X VALUE-DESTROYING")

beats.narration_text: Three paired bar groups compare inside view versus reference-class-adjusted estimates. Revenue synergies collapse from 120 to 54 million. Integration costs rise from 40 to 52 million. Adjusted max price is 920 million versus a deal price of 1.1 billion.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_DebiasComparison: paired bar groups showing inside view vs. reference-class-adjusted values.

beats.narration_text: Adjusted max price is 920 million. The deal is at 1.1 billion. Value-destroying at reference-class rates.

props.command: claude "What revenue realization rate would make this deal break even at $1.1B? And what is the historical p75 realization rate for revenue synergies?"

beats.narration_text: Breakeven requires 91 percent revenue realization. Historical p75 is 60 percent. The deal requires top-decile execution just to break even.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- Breakeven revenue realization: 91%. Historical p75: 60%. Top decile required just to break even.

beats.narration_text: The teardown: the inside view is not analysis. It is optimism. Replace it with the base rate before you sign a term sheet.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- inside view = optimism, not analysis. Base rates are the honest answer.

beats.narration_text: Next: build a full reference class database from public M&A disclosures. Follow for more corporate finance teardowns.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps: build reference class database from public deal data.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
